snapshot.title: The Turtles and the Floor
snapshot.one_sentence_claim: This paper argues that the Cross/Pentecost sequence can be modeled as irreversible coupling-architecture modification, with stated formal, empirical, and theological constraints.

maturity.label: Formal Model
maturity.boundary: Lean verifies internal structure only; empirical and theological claims remain separately tested.

facts.frame: The anomaly is missing composition, not a missing component.
facts.admit: Dual-substrate model is speculative; key parameter derivation is incomplete; categorical construction is incomplete.
facts.claim: The sequence is structurally coherent under irreversible coupling modification.
facts.test: Check symmetry-breaking, energy accounting, parameter derivation, EM invisibility, historical discontinuity.
facts.snap: One fatal kill condition breaks the model; multiple prediction failures collapse it.

seven_q.q0_posture: Structural correspondence is tested, not assumed.
seven_q.q1_identity: The object is a claimed substrate-transition model.
seven_q.q3_claim: Irreversible coupling modification explains the sequence.
seven_q.q4_support: Formal structure, analogy class, evidence hooks.
seven_q.q5_dependencies: Requires prior framework layers and valid physical operation.
seven_q.q6_consequences: If true, theology and physics share constrained architecture.
seven_q.q7_kill_conditions: Reversibility, failed conservation, failed parameter derivation, categorical contradiction.

not_claimed: Lean proves the external reality of the event.
